title: DESIGN.md §9 violation: api.rs and jmap.rs store sent_at as RFC 3339 not SQLite datetime format
description: api.rs:131 and jmap.rs:2124 call to_rfc3339() for sent_at passed to insert_message/insert_message_ext. DESIGN.md §9 only names expires_at and created_at, so this is not a strict §9 violation. However sent_at rows from relay-delivered messages arrive in the sender's format (which may be SQLite format), creating mixed formats in the same column. sent_at is never used in SQL datetime() comparisons so no functional breakage, but cross-row sorting by sent_at could give wrong order when formats are mixed. Severity: low.
notes: 2026-04-26 round-66 assessment: Not worth fixing with a normalization helper. sent_at is display-only; no SQL date arithmetic is performed on it anywhere in the codebase. Normalization would add code complexity (a helper, migration, or on-read transform) with no functional benefit. Leave as informational tracking; the mixed-format cosmetic issue is acceptable at current usage.
